rulings.Case Title: ANGELA LEUNG VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LP DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND and SUSTAINED in part, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Request for Judicial notice is granted. No opposition filed to the request for judicial notice. 1. Demurrer to cause of action for breach of contract is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff's claim that she did not agree to a new effective date must be accepted as true on a demurrer. Section 23 of the Lease does not release the former owner from liability for the wrongs/breaches committed prior to the transfer. However, Judicial Notice is taken of the SAC. The Court sustains Defendant's request and disregards the inconsistent allegations in paragraph 34 of the SAC and paragraph 31 in the TAC. Plaintiff must amend paragraph 31 of the TAC to reflect the prior language, specifically the word 'acquiesced,' used in paragraph 34 of the SAC. 2. Demurrer as to the cause of action for Tortious Breach of Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for P to allege either an independent duty arising out of Tort law; or to amend the cause of action to a contractual Breach of good faith and request the appropriate relief. 3. Demurrer as to the cause of action for Quiet Enjoyment is SUTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The implied covenant of quiet possession and enjoyment included in every lease is breached upon actual or constructive eviction of the tenant. Cal. Civ. Code § 1927. Plaintiff's allegations regarding Defendant's interference with respect to her tenancy of suite 432 do not rise to a constructive eviction or an actual eviction. 4. Demurrer to cause of action for UCL violation is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for plaintiff to allege facts constituting unfair, unlawful, or fraudulent practices within the meaning of UCL and with requisite specificity. Plaintiff must allege the unfair, unlawful, or fraudulent practice specifically as it pertains to Stonestown Shopping Center and allege facts that support a claim for restitution. The amendment must show on its face that the claim is not time-barred. Plaintiff's Request to file a Sur-Reply is DENIED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA LEUNG VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT STONESTOWN MEDICAL BUILDING, LLC. MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED in part, and DENIED in part. Defendant's motion to strike emotional distress damages is MOOT. Defendant's motion to strike punitive damages is DENIED. Plaintiff may be entitled to punitive damages under her UCL claim. Defendant's motion to strike statutory damages is GRANTED. Defendant's motion to strike damages for destruction of and loss of use of personal property is GRANTED. Defendant's motion to strike Restitutionary Damages for Plaintiff's cost and expenses during her effort to comply with amendment to move to suite 425 is GRANTED. Defendant's Motion to strike Declaratory Relief is MOOT. Defendant's Motion to strike Attorney's fees is DENIED. Plaintiff's Request to file a Sur-Reply is DENIED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA LEUNG VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT STONESTOWN MEDICAL BUILDING, LLC. DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND and SUSTAINED in part, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. 1. Demurrer as to the cause of action for Quiet Enjoyment is SUTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The implied covenant of quiet possession and enjoyment included in every lease is breached upon actual or constructive eviction of the tenant. Cal. Civ. Code § 1927. Plaintiff's allegations regarding Defendant's interference with respect to her tenancy of suite 432 do not rise to a constructive eviction or an actual eviction. 2. Demurrer to cause of action for UCL violation is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for plaintiff to allege the unfair, unlawful, or fraudulent practice specifically as it pertains to Stonestown Medical Building and with requisite specificity. A UCL "fraud" prong claim or claims involving misrepresentation requires proof of actual reliance on the allegedly deceptive or misleading statements. Plaintiff must allege facts that support a claim for restitution and an injunction. Plaintiff must show that Defendant had an affirmative duty to disclose to Plaintiff whether there were any additional units for lease in the building and that Plaintiff was entitled to relocation to any unit in the building. The amendment must show on its face that the claim is not time-barred. 3. Demurrer to Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The claim is barred by the statute of limitations. The fact that Plaintiff could not move into her new unit she rented because she was remodeling it and that she had to pay for two rents does not save this claim from the SOL because it does not allege any conduct on the part of Defendant that does not solely relate back to Defendant's failure to honor the lease addendum. The IIED claim is based on a breach of contract and as such the SOL began to run in 2014, or at the latest in February 2016. Because P filed her claim in June 2018, the 2 year SOL has run. 4. Demurrer to Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. This claim is barred by the statute of limitations. Plaintiff's claim that Defendant was negligent in complying with the obligations under the lease and Amendment began to run in October 2014, or at the latest in February 2016 when Plaintiff secured a lease at another location. Additionally, California does not recognize an independent tort for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Instead, recovery of emotional distress damages is premised on defendant's negligence (i.e., breach of a duty) that proximately causes emotional distress. Plaintiff fails to state a COA because there is no independent duty aside from D's contractual duties. Accordingly, P's claim for NIED is a breach of contract claim and Plaintiff cannot recover emotional distress damages for negligence. 5. Demurrer as to the claim for Declaratory Relief is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. P moved out of the property in August 2018. Whether Plaintiff has a right to terminate the lease is a determination that will be made under the breach of contract claim either through an offset to her claim for damages or through a cross-complaint. Plaintiff's Request to file a Sur-Reply is DENIED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FAST CAPITAL, LLC. ET AL VS. LA COSTA LOANS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss The Entire Action Pursuant To C.C.P. Sec 581 (F) (2)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT SS DREAMBUILDERS, LLC, CHONG HONG Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss The Entire Action Pursuant To C.C.P. Sec 581 (F) (2) GRANTED, in part. Request for Judicial notice is granted. No opposition filed to the request for judicial notice. Dismissal with prejudice will be with respect to Defendants Sophie Han, Chong Han, and SS Dreambuilders, LLC only. Plaintiff failed to comply with the Court's May 28th Order requiring Plaintiff to provide the Court with a curtesy copy of its opposition no later than May 29 and with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Plaintiff's opposition was received on May 31 and without a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Defendant may request attorney fees and costs in by a separate motion. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FAST CAPITAL, LLC. ET AL VS. LA COSTA LOANS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Motion For Relief From Order Striking Second Amended Complaint; Declaration Of Moichael S. Traylor, Esq Pursuant To Ccp Sec 473
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF FAST CAPITAL, LLC., PAUL ARAND, ROBERT FRAZIER, ROBERT VANDERWALL, KRISTIN KEENCE, DANIEL KIM, CA CAPITAL FUNDS FOR 426-428 17TH AVE TRUST 092013, CA CAPITAL FUNDS FOR 284 ROOSEVELT WAY TRUST, ROES 1-20 Plaintiffs' Motion For Relief From Order Striking Second Amended Complaint; Declaration Of Moichael S. Traylor, Esq Pursuant To Ccp Sec 473 OFF CALENDAR. Moving party failed to comply with the Court's May 28th Order requiring Plaintiff to provide the Court with curtesy copies of the moving papers no later than May 29 and with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Plaintiff's moving papers were received on May 31 and without a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Plaintiff may re-file a noticed motion. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID TSAO VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Awarding Reasonable Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 7, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Awarding Reasonable Attorney Fees GRANTED. Defendant is the prevailing party. Per the attorney fee provision on p.6 of the lease, Defendant is awarded to reasonable attorney fees and costs. The amount awarded is $5,100. = (501/CFH)
